metadata.title:
COUNCIL PR0DS


RALSTON

BRIDGE ESTIMATES ORDERED
FAIL TO APPEAR, AND MON
ROE STREET AND OTHER
STRUCTURES ARE STILL
TIED UP.

pages.raw_text:
For some reason City Engineer
Ralston seems to be delaying the
duty of supplying the city council
with needed information as to the
relative cost of concrete and steel
bridges. The record was produced
in the city council last night to
show that Mr.. Ralston had on July
Is been ordered to prepare plans
and furnish estimates for a con-
crete bridge at Monroe street, on
a motion introduced by Councilman
Schiller.


No report having been made by
Mr.. Ralston, the matter was again
brought up in the city council last
night in a resolution introduced by
Councilman Dalke, and Mr.. Ralston
was again ordered to furnish this
information, and also an estimate
on steel bridge, without which
the city council is up in the air,
figuratively speaking, on the bridge
question.


Councilman Mohr seemed to
think it was useless to ask the
engineer to make an estimate on
the Monroe street bridge when he
had already expressed the belief
that it will cost about 100000
Mohr favord prodding the engineer
along If he thought it would result
in any good, but he seemed to
think it would not. He thought
another resolution month hence
would be necessary before plans
are secured.


Dalke said If the engineer will
not work, the council should get
one that will. Mr.. Reuterdahl, OF
the city engineers force, could pre-
pare Monroe street bridge plans il
the council ordered him to do so
Mr.. Dalke said. I'VE have been
shooting into the air long enough
and ii is time that the city council
get busy and get some tangible
information on the bridge ques
tion," said Dalke. The resolution
of Dalke was adopted, with the un
derstanding that ii Engineer Rails
ton had gone to work on Schiller'f
concrete bridge request, he need
not pay any attention to the con
crete portion OF Dalke's motion.


Councilman Phillips, from the
presiding officers chair, announced
early In the evening that all con
sideration of bridges had been post
poned until the meeting of the
committee OF the whole next Tues
day night, but the discussion or
the Dalke resolution cropped out
